ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
ant: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Antakarinya.
any: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anyi.
ayin: noun. The sixteenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
nay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngarrindjeri.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
qat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for reserved for local use.
qua: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Quapaw.
quaint: adjective. Of a person: cunning, crafty.
quantity: noun. A fundamental, generic term used when referring to the measurement (count, amount) of a scalar, vector, number of items or to some
quay: noun. A stone or concrete structure on navigable water used for loading and unloading vessels; a wharf.
quint: noun. An interval of one fifth.
quinta: noun. A country house in Madeira.
quit: adjective. Released from obligation, penalty, etc; free, clear, or rid.
taint: noun. A contamination, decay or putrefaction, especially in food.
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Upper Tanana.
taunt: verb. To make fun of (someone); to goad (a person) into responding, often in an aggressive manner. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
taut: adjective. Under tension, like a stretched bowstring, rope, or sail; tight.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titan: noun. Something or someone of very large stature, greatness, or godliness.
tut: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Altaic languages.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
unity: noun. Oneness: the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
uta: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Otank.
yanqui: noun. A citizen of the United States of America, as opposed to a Latin American.
yin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yinchia.
yuan: noun. The basic unit of money in China.
